info.title: wtc query API
info.description: Versioned read API served by `wtc serve`. The portal SPA generates its typed client from this document. Ingest endpoints (/ingest/*) are source-authenticated (HMAC / bearer / shared-secret header) and out of scope for the portal client.

servers.description: The wtc server, relative to its own origin.

get.summary: Liveness probe (unauthenticated).
Validate the supplied bearer token.
The server's build-stamped version (bearer-authed — versions fingerprint deployments).
List change events, newest first, cursor-paginated.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Facet OR-set (comma-separated): github/gitlab/flux/argocd/… or mapping-webhook names.

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: Exact git sha/revision OR-set (comma-separated) — used to scope the timeline to one changeset.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a prior response's next_cursor.

get.summary: Trace a commit/tag across builds, intents and per-env applies.

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: A git sha (short or long), image tag, or artifact ref.

get.summary: Compare what is deployed across two environments, current or as of a past instant.

parameters.name: at
parameters.description: Point-in-time: reconstruct the comparison as of this RFC3339 instant. Omitted => current state.

get.summary: Summarize deploy activity since an instant (shift-handoff view).
List the changes in a window before an instant or an event.

parameters.name: ts
parameters.description: Anchor instant. One of ts or id is required.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Anchor on an event id (e.g. an alert). One of ts or id is required.

get.summary: Rank the changes most likely to have caused an alert (or list the alerts following a change).
get.description: Anchored on an alert event or a bare instant, scores the changes in the preceding window as suspects — a deterministic documented heuristic: recency, same env (hard signal), same service, kind weight, failed/degraded bump. Anchored on a non-alert change the direction flips: it lists the alerts that fired in the window after it.

parameters.name: ts
parameters.description: Anchor instant. One of ts or id is required.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Anchor on an event id (an alert for causes; a change for effects). One of ts or id is required.

parameters.name: env
parameters.description: Scoring env context; overrides the anchor event's env. Required with a bare ts anchor to enable the same-env signal.

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: Scoring service context; overrides the anchor event's service.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Lookback (causes) / lookahead (effects) duration (Go format, e.g. 2h). Default 2h.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum suspects returned. Default 20, capped at 100.

get.summary: Report which rule set each inferred field of an event.
get.description: Replays the CURRENT rules (DB overrides included) over the event's recorded ingest-time facts with a first-writer-wins trace: per field, the winning rule (index + match spec), 'normalizer' when the source parser pre-filled it, or 'unmatched'. Rows without recorded facts (pre-migration, or generic/record/wrap sources) report facts_recorded=false — never a guess.

get.summary: Stream the filtered ledger as CSV, NDJSON or a JSON array.
get.description: Audit/compliance export — 'every prod change in Q3'. Filters mirror /events; ordering is ts DESC. CSV carries the flat columns in a stable append-only order; ndjson and json carry full events including payload and facts. Streams page-by-page, so large ranges never buffer server-side.

get.summary: Stream a consistent snapshot of the sqlite ledger.
get.description: A point-in-time VACUUM INTO snapshot taken while serving (WAL-safe, compacted), streamed as a sqlite database file. The postgres backend answers 501 — back it up with pg_dump instead (docs/setup/backup.md).

501.description: Not supported by the postgres backend.

get.summary: Report ingest health, source watermarks and unmapped events.
Event counts over time (gap-filled), for the activity chart.
Per-environment deploy frequency, failures and health.
Change-failure rate and MTTR over a window (overall, per env, per owner).

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Deploy→failure attribution span (Go duration, e.g. 60m). Default 1h.

get.summary: Logical changes in a window: build → merge → per-env deploys collapsed by app sha.
Distinct env/service/actor values for filter dropdowns.
Services × environments deploy grid (diff visualized), current or as of a past instant.

parameters.name: envs
parameters.description: Ordered comma-separated column list. Omitted => distinct non-ephemeral envs.

parameters.name: at
parameters.description: Point-in-time: reconstruct the grid as of this RFC3339 instant. Omitted => current state.

get.summary: The normalization rules and tag patterns in effect.

put.summary: Replace the inference rules (validated, persisted, hot-reloaded).

delete.summary: Drop the rules override; revert to the YAML baseline.

put.summary: Replace the tag patterns (validated, persisted, hot-reloaded).

delete.summary: Drop the tag-patterns override; revert to the YAML baseline.

get.summary: Server-sent events: newly-stored events pushed live.
get.description: text/event-stream. Each `data:` frame is one Event (as in /events). Consume with fetch — EventSource cannot set the Authorization header.

200.description: An SSE stream of Event frames.

bearerAuth.description: A value from the server's auth.api_tokens.

env.description: Empty string means unmapped (surfaced by doctor).

repo.description: Source repo (owner/name); empty for cluster-side events (flux/argo).

owner.description: Owning team, inferred from service via the catalog; empty when unmapped.

payload.description: Redacted raw JSON; may be omitted.

facts.description: Redacted ingest-time rule facts + pre-rules snapshot (JSON, for /explain); absent when not recorded.

next_cursor.description: Pass as ?cursor= to fetch the next page; absent on the last page.

score.description: Deterministic suspicion score; higher = likelier. Relative within one report.

reasons.description: Human-readable score breakdown (recency, same env/service, kind, status).

direction.description: causes: changes before an alert/instant. effects: alerts after a change.

env.description: Env used for the same-env signal; absent when unknown (signal disabled, noted).

service.description: Service used for the same-service signal.

suspects.description: Ranked best-first. Always an array, never null.

notes.description: Explicit caveats — disabled signals, truncation, empty windows.

field.description: env | cluster | namespace | service | kind | actor.

origin.description: rule: a rules-engine entry set it. normalizer: the source parser pre-filled it. unmatched: nothing set it.

rule_index.description: Zero-based index into the effective rules list; present when origin=rule.

rule_match.description: Compact match spec of the winning rule, e.g. 'source=flux namespace=prod-*'.

facts_recorded.description: False for rows ingested before the facts migration or via sources that set fields directly; traces are then absent.

notes.description: Caveats — unrecorded facts, rules drift since ingest.

RuleFacts.description: The ingest-time facts the rules engine matched against (recorded, redacted).

sha.description: Longest known form of the resolved sha.

a.description: Artifact (fallback ref) in env A; empty = not deployed.

drift_seconds.description: |aTS - bTS| when both exist and differ.

only_in.description: Env name when deployed to exactly one side.

revision_only.description: Compared refs because artifact data was missing.

new_services.description: First ever seen inside the window.

unowned_services.description: Services absent from the catalog; only when a catalog is in use.

webhook_churn.description: Mapping-webhook sources with a probable unstable dedup_key.

ts.description: Bucket start (UTC): YYYY-MM-DD for day, YYYY-MM-DDThh:00 for hour.

last_ts.description: Most recent deploy in the window.

last_status.description: Status of the most recent deploy (env-health signal).

envs.description: Non-empty envs only, sorted by name.

failures.description: Deploys that failed or were followed by an alert/rollback in the same env within the window.

mttr_seconds.description: Mean alert firing→resolved duration; absent when no incident resolved.

samples.description: Changes that reached this env in the window.

median_seconds.description: Median build/merge→deploy lead time; absent when no samples.

p90_seconds.description: 90th-percentile lead time; absent when no samples.

lead_time.description: Pipeline lead time (build/merge→deploy) per env: median + p90.

sha.description: Longest known form of the app commit sha.

envs.description: Envs with a succeeded deploy of this sha.

refs.description: Every ref in the change; filters the timeline to exactly its events.

cells.description: Keyed by env; a missing env means not deployed there.

RuleMatch.description: Selector; empty fields are unconstrained. Strings support * and ** globs.

RuleSet.description: Fields a rule sets. Values are Go templates over the fact map.

version.description: Build-stamped binary version ("dev" for unstamped builds).

MaskedSecret.description: Constant "********" when the secret is configured, "" when not. Never the value, never a partial, length-independent.

capture_enabled.description: Raw ingest bodies are being written to disk (dev capture mode) — surface as a warning.

ConfigStorage.description: Storage backend; for postgres the DSN's location parts with credentials stripped (absent when the DSN does not parse).

api_tokens.description: One mask per configured token — count visible, values never.

repos.description: Poller scope; empty with poller_enabled means auto-discovery. Entries may be globs.

ConfigScopeMatch.description: Ingest allow/deny selector; empty fields are unconstrained, string fields are globs (* one segment, ** any depth).

ConfigScope.description: Ingest allow/deny lists. Deny wins over allow; empty allow = allow all; empty deny = deny none.

ConfigWebhook.description: One mapping webhook: auth shape with the secret masked, templates in full.

mapping.description: Effective (preset-resolved) field templates, shown in full.

listen.description: Separate unauthenticated /metrics listener address; "" = not open.

ConfigResponse.description: Effective config: live-editable normalization (rules/tag_patterns) plus the redacted static snapshot of server/storage/auth/sources/jobs. Secrets are constant "********" masks — values never leave the server.

rules_overridden.description: true when rules come from a DB override rather than the YAML baseline.
